title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Finally, your surgeon will remove the catheters and close the incisions in your groin. The procedure will generally take 2-3 hours.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside your arteries, narrowing the passageway and interfering with blood flow. This arterial blockage is called stenosis.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
text_en: Insufficient blood flow to a tissue generally causes pain.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty. A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty, or NC-PTA, to restore blood flow in an artery block by an atherosclerotic plaque.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: To begin the procedure, you'll be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope. A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted. Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die. This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis. Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially re-establishing blood flow.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body. It consists of four chambers.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en: The upper chambers are the right and left atria. The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
text_en:
The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles, the right ventricle connects to the pulmonary artery, and the left ventricle to the aorta. Those are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

---

**切分依据：**

| Block | 画面动作 | 句数 |
|-------|---------|------|
| 1 | 心脏整体外观展示（器官概述 + 四腔总览） | 2句 |
| 2 | 分别标注/高亮四个腔室（上方心房、下方心室） | 2句 |
| 3 | 展示腔室间连接通路及瓣膜位置（房→室→动脉，单向血流） | 2句 |

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta. When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta. When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: A normal aortic valve has three smooth cusps that open widely and close tightly.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: For aortic valve may be leaky or narrowed, causing your heart to work harder and disrupt your blood flow.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: A leaky valve does not close all the way, causing blood to leak back into the ventricle between each heartbeat. This condition is called aortic regurgitation.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: A narrowed aortic valve does not open all the way, which restricts the amount of blood passing out of the ventricle into the aorta. This condition is called aortic stenosis.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: You may have been born with an abnormal aortic valve. For example, your aortic valve may only have two cusps instead of three, so it may not open and close properly.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: Your aortic valve may have become damaged during your lifetime. A condition called acquired aortic valve disease. Cusps include rheumatic fever, which can be a complication of untreated strep throat. Andocarditis, which is a bacterial infection of the valve, and sclerosis or hardening of the aortic leaflets.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened. Depending on the nature of the valve's defect, the walls of the left ventricle may become abnormally thick or thin, and the problem can eventually affect the right side of the heart as well.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: And aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be started in your arm to provide fluids and medications.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: A catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain urine.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Aortic valve replacements are done under general anesthesia. This means you will be asleep for the duration of the operation. Add a tube will be placed through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during surgery.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest. The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective membrane encircling the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: At this point, your heart will be connected to a heart lung machine. Tubes are used to reroute your blood into this machine, which takes over the functions of the heart and lungs during the operation.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
text_en: Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and the blood inside removed. This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.
